mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in industrial production indicates improving real activity and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the signal is observed.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month INDPRO growth has a nonpositive coefficient for 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if positive-growth months do not outperform negative-growth months by at least 25 bp on average.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high and still rising, discount-rate pressure dominates near-term earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% and rises by more than 0.25pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after the unemployment trigger are positive in more than 60% of cases or if the return difference versus nontriggered months is statistically insignificant at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and payrolls both expand over 3 months, household income and spending reinforce each other, raising expected revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS and PAYEMS condition fails to outperform cases where only one series is positive, or if its walk-forward 20-day mean return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts signals weakening credit-sensitive demand and future construction activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing-start declines are followed by average 60-day S&P 500 returns equal to or above unconditional average returns, or if downside hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate is already elevated, investors price tighter policy and lower equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS is above 4%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration and high FEDFUNDS interaction does not have a negative coefficient, or if triggered 20-day returns exceed unconditional returns in more than half of walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth with a low unemployment rate signals late-cycle wage and margin pressure rather than early-cycle recovery, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE is below 5%
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS growth and low UNRATE interaction produces nonnegative 5-day forward return spreads versus unconditional returns over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth confirms broad demand expansion and supports aggregate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if observations with GDPC1 growth above 2% do not show higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than observations below 2%, or if the outperformance spread is negative in most walk-forward folds.
